channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Tech law in 2025: a look ahead at AI, privacy and social media regulation under the new Trump administration
item.description: Artificial intelligence harms, problematic social media content, data privacy violations – the issues are the same, but the policymakers and regulators who deal with them are about to change.
item.dc:creator: Sylvia Lu, University of Michigan, ABC 4

item.title: Legacy of Jan. 6 prosecutions in flux with Trump presidency on horizon
item.description: The Justice Department’s prosecution of the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack — hailed as one of the “largest, most complex, and most resource-intensive investigations” in its history — could soon go out with a fizzle.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In-N-Out opening new restaurants in 2025, and one is in Utah
item.description: The burger chain, which does not franchise its restaurants, currently keeps its operations (mostly) to the Western half of the country.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: British man and a nursing assistant were the last victims identified in New Orleans attack
item.description: The coroner's office said all the victims died from blunt force injuries. The suspect in the attack, Shamsud-Din Jabbar, was fatally shot in a firefight with police.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Golden Globes: What to look for and how to watch on Sunday night
item.description: Hollywood is getting dressed up as the Golden Globes return for their annual champagne-soaked celebration of film and television that serves as the ceremonial start to awards season.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Data company figures out which airlines are most on time
item.description: Mexican airline Aeromexico had the world's best record for on-time arrivals in 2024, according to an annual ranking released Thursday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: The heaviest snowfall in a decade is possible as a wintry blast roils parts of the US
item.description: A blast of snow, ice, wind and plunging temperatures stirred up dangerous travel conditions in parts of the central U.S. on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth And Brian Witte, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Lawmakers brace for Trump's promised Jan. 6 pardons. Some are urging restraint
item.description: The fourth anniversary of the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol has a new focus as lawmakers brace for the prospect that Donald Trump may soon pardon many of the more than 1,500 people charged with crimes for their actions related to the riot.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Arizona death row inmate wants to be executed earlier than the state is planning
item.description: An Arizona death row inmate called on the state Supreme Court to skip legal formalities and carry out his death sentence earlier than officials were planning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's funeral begins by tracing 100 years from rural Georgia to the world stage
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Jimmy Carter 's extended public farewell began Saturday in Georgia, with the 39th U.S. president’s flag-draped casket tracing his long arc from the Depression-era South and family farming business to the pinnacle of American political power and decades as a global humanitarian.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, JEFF AMY and KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral has started. Here's what to know
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter began Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: BILL BARROW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Suspected illegal immigrant's fake bomb threat shuts down SC highway
item.description: A suspected illegal immigrant was arrested in South Carolina after officials say he made a fake bomb threat during a traffic stop which ultimately led to a section of the I-85 being shut down for nearly five hours.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: LeBron James breaks Michael Jordan's record for 30-point games with his 563rd
item.description: LeBron James broke Michael Jordan's NBA record for 30-point games during the Los Angeles Lakers' victory over Atlanta on Friday night.
item.dc:creator: GREG BEACHAM, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Los Altos woman who plotted to kill estranged husband makes bid for parole
item.description: (KRON) -- A Los Altos woman who attempted to kill her estranged husband while the couple was going through a bitter divorce will appear in front of a state parole board Friday asking for freedom.
item.dc:creator: Amy Larson, NewsNation

item.title: Judge sets Trump's sentencing in hush money case for Jan. 10, but signals no jail time
item.description: In an extraordinary turn, a judge Friday set President-elect Donald Trump's sentencing in his hush money case for Jan. 10 — little over a week before he's due to return to the White House — but promised not to jail him.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Three members of Wagner family sentenced for Pike County massacre
item.description: WAVERLY, Ohio (WCMH) – Three members of the Wagner family learned their fate on Friday for their involvement in the Rhoden family massacre.
item.dc:creator: Ava Boldizar, NewsNation

item.title: Republican Mike Johnson reelected as House speaker after dramatic floor vote
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Mike Johnson won reelection to the House speakership on a first ballot Friday, pushing past GOP hard-right holdouts and buoyed with a nod of support from President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, FARNOUSH AMIRI and KEVIN FREKING Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Nearly 700 Party City store leases heading to auction
item.description: Nearly 700 Party City store leases will be heading to auction next month, according to an announcement Friday.
item.dc:creator: Mac Bell, NewsNation

item.title: John Capodice, actor who appeared in 'Ace Ventura,' 'Seinfeld' and more, dies at 83
item.description: News of Capodice’s death on Monday was publicly announced in an obituary posted by the Pizzi Funeral Home in Northvale, New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: How to donate funds, blood after fatal Bourbon Street attack
item.description: As the City of New Orleans begins to recover from a fatal attack on Bourbon Street on New Year's Day, efforts are underway to help victims and their families.
item.dc:creator: Bella Dardano, ABC 4

item.title: Republicans' trust in accuracy of US elections jumps after Trump's win, AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A majority of Republicans say they are confident in the 2024 vote count after Donald Trump’s win, according to a new poll that finds a sharp turnaround from GOP voters’ skepticism about U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINLEY SANDERS and NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Federal courts won't refer Clarence Thomas to attorney general over ethics
item.description: Thomas has agreed to follow updated requirements on reporting trips and gifts, including clearer guidelines on hospitality from friends.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Surgeon general calls for cancer warnings on alcohol
item.description: Alcoholic beverages should carry a prominent warning label about the link between alcohol and cancer, according to the U.S. surgeon general.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KRON4

item.title: FBI releases new video of suspect planting pipe bomb on eve of Capitol riot
item.description: A harrowing chapter in American history remains shrouded in mystery: Who planted pipe bombs outside offices of the Democratic and Republican national committees in Washington on the eve of the attack on the Capitol?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: An orca that carried her dead calf for weeks in 2018 is doing so once again
item.description: An endangered Pacific Northwest orca that made global headlines in 2018 for carrying her dead calf for over two weeks is doing so once again following the death of her new calf, in another sign of grief over lost offspring, researchers said.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Chief Justice John Roberts defends judicial independence, says it is under threat in several ways
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Chief Justice John Roberts issued a defense Tuesday of judicial independence, which he said is under threat from intimidation, disinformation and the prospect of public officials defying court orders. Roberts laid out his concerns in his annual report on the federal judiciary.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI found 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home during search in December, prosecutors say
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Federal agents found one of the largest stockpiles of homemade explosives they have ever seized when they arrested a Virginia man on a firearms charge last month, according to a court filing by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Sugar Bowl security to equal Super Bowl: New Orleans police
item.description: The New Orleans Police Department revealed Thursday that the annual Sugar Bowl will feature heightened security after a man drove directly into a crowd on Bourbon Street earlier this week, leaving 15 dead.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5
